articles.title: Sabrina Carpenter Turns Coachella Into Sabrinawood
articles.summary: Sabrina Carpenter headlined Coachella's main stage with a full Old Hollywood themed set featuring Susan Sarandon, Samuel L. Jackson, Will Ferrell, Sam Elliott, and Corey Fogelmanis. It was one of the most star-packed festival performances in recent memory and cemented Carpenter as a genuine pop superstar.
articles.content:
Sabrina Carpenter promised she would be back on Coachella's main stage, and she absolutely delivered. Her set was built around a concept she called Sabrinawood, a full Old Hollywood fantasy world complete with glamour, theatrics, and an absurd amount of famous faces showing up to play along.

The guest list alone was enough to make your jaw drop. Susan Sarandon, Samuel L. Jackson, Will Ferrell, Sam Elliott, and Corey Fogelmanis all made appearances during the show. There were also men dressed as dogs, because of course there were. Carpenter has always had a sharp comedic edge and a flair for the theatrical, and this set put both on full display in front of one of the biggest live audiences a pop artist can get.

Carpenter first performed at Coachella as a supporting act and openly said she wanted to come back as a headliner. Now she has done exactly that, and then some. Sabrinawood was not just a concert. It was a full statement of intent from one of pop's most exciting names right now.
keyPoints: Sabrina Carpenter headlined Coachella's main stage with an Old Hollywood themed show called Sabrinawood
Susan Sarandon, Samuel L. Jackson, Will Ferrell, Sam Elliott, and Corey Fogelmanis all appeared on stage
Carpenter had previously promised to return to Coachella as a headliner after performing in a supporting role
The set featured theatrical costumes and men dressed as dogs, leaning into Carpenter's comedic persona
This performance is being called one of the most memorable Coachella sets in years

articles.title: Rory McIlroy Breaks Masters Record With Six-Shot Lead
articles.summary: Rory McIlroy fired a 7-under 65 on Friday at the Masters, closing with six birdies over his last seven holes to build the largest 36-hole lead in Masters history at six shots. It is the kind of dominant performance that has the golf world holding its breath.
articles.content:
Rory McIlroy is playing like a man on a mission at Augusta National. After round two of the 2026 Masters, McIlroy sits at 7-under par after shooting a stunning 65 on Friday. He closed his round with six birdies across his last seven holes. That is the kind of finish that makes highlights reels and gives opponents nightmares.

The Masters, held every April at Augusta National Golf Club in Georgia, is one of golf's four major championships. It is the one McIlroy has famously never won, despite winning every other major. That storyline has followed him for over a decade and makes every Masters he plays feel like must-watch television.

A six-shot lead after 36 holes is the largest in Masters history. That is not just good golf. That is a record. The tournament still has two rounds to go and Augusta has a habit of dramatic finishes, but right now Rory McIlroy looks like he is finally ready to complete the career grand slam.
keyPoints: Rory McIlroy shot a 7-under 65 in round two of the 2026 Masters
He closed with six birdies over his last seven holes
His six-shot lead is the largest after 36 holes in Masters history
McIlroy has never won the Masters despite winning every other major
Two rounds remain but McIlroy looks firmly in control

articles.title: Katseye and Huntr/x Perform Golden Together at Coachella
articles.summary: K-pop group Katseye was joined by a surprise trio at Coachella when Ejae, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami, the real singers behind the animated group Huntr/x from the film KPop Demon Hunters, appeared on stage to perform the song Golden. The moment landed extra hard because Huntr/x just won the Oscar for best original song.
articles.content:
Katseye was already having a big Coachella set when things got even bigger. Ejae, who voices the character Rumi, Audrey Nuna, who voices Mira, and Rei Ami, who voices Zoey, all came out on stage as the real people behind Huntr/x, the animated K-pop group from the film KPop Demon Hunters. Together with Katseye they performed Golden, the breakout song from the film.

The timing could not have been better. Huntr/x won the Academy Award for best original song for Golden just weeks ago, so the Coachella crowd knew exactly what they were hearing. KPop Demon Hunters is an animated film that features a fictional K-pop group called Huntr/x, and Golden is the song that became a cultural moment well beyond the film itself. Seeing the voices behind the characters perform it live is something fans have wanted since the Oscar win.

Coachella surprises live and die by how well they land in the room and online. This one landed. Katseye is one of the most exciting new K-pop acts around right now, and sharing a stage with the Huntr/x singers while performing an Oscar-winning song is exactly the kind of crossover moment that defines a generation of pop culture.
keyPoints: Ejae, Audrey Nuna, and Rei Ami surprised the Coachella crowd by joining Katseye on stage
They are the singers behind Huntr/x, the animated group from KPop Demon Hunters
Together they performed Golden, which recently won the Oscar for best original song
Katseye is one of the most prominent new K-pop acts performing on the global stage
The moment combined live K-pop performance with an Oscar-winning animated film world

articles.title: Sam Altman Confirms Molotov Cocktail Story in Blog Post
articles.summary: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman published a personal blog post on Friday responding to a major New Yorker investigation by journalists Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz, confirming a past incident involving a Molotov cocktail and addressing other claims in the piece. This is one of the most candid public statements Altman has ever made about his personal history.
articles.content:
Sam Altman, the CEO of OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT, posted a lengthy personal blog response on Friday to a New Yorker investigation written by Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz. The investigation, which Altman described as incendiary, dug into his past and his leadership. Rather than go quiet, Altman went public and addressed it head on.

One of the most striking moments in his post was confirming that a Molotov cocktail incident from his past actually happened. A Molotov cocktail is a handmade incendiary device, and the fact that Altman confirmed being involved in any incident connected to one is the kind of detail that stops you mid-scroll. He also said he wanted to own his past mistakes without hiding from them.

Sam Altman is arguably the most influential person in tech right now. OpenAI's tools are used by hundreds of millions of people. So when the person running that company writes a confessional blog post confirming things a major magazine said about him, it matters far beyond Silicon Valley gossip. This one will have a long tail.
keyPoints: Sam Altman published a personal blog post responding to a New Yorker investigation by Ronan Farrow and Andrew Marantz
Altman confirmed a past incident involving a Molotov cocktail
He said he wanted to acknowledge his past mistakes openly
Altman is the CEO of OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT
This is one of the most personal and candid public statements Altman has ever made

articles.title: Bini Make History as First Filipino Group at Coachella
articles.summary: Bini took the Coachella 2026 stage on Friday night, becoming the first Filipino group ever to perform at the iconic California desert festival. It is a massive moment for Filipino pop music on the global stage.
articles.content:
Bini did something no Filipino group has ever done before. The eight-member girl group stepped onto the Coachella stage on Friday night and performed for a crowd that stretches across livestreams and social media to millions of fans worldwide. Coachella, held annually in Indio, California, is one of the most culturally watched music festivals on the planet. Getting a slot there is a stamp of legitimacy that artists chase for years.

Bini have been building momentum in the Philippines and across Southeast Asia with their brand of Filipino pop, known locally as P-pop. Think tight choreography, catchy hooks, and a sound that blends local flavor with the slick production of modern pop. They have become a symbol of what Filipino music can look like when it gets a global platform.

This performance puts Bini in the same conversation as other Asian acts who used Coachella to announce themselves to the world. Fans are already calling it a historic night. If you have not listened to Bini yet, now is the perfect time to start.
keyPoints: Bini are the first Filipino group ever to perform at Coachella
The performance happened on Friday night at Coachella 2026
Bini perform Filipino pop, also known as P-pop
Coachella is one of the most watched music festivals in the world
This puts Filipino music on a truly global stage

articles.title: Susan Sarandon Surprises Coachella With Sabrina Carpenter
articles.summary: Susan Sarandon made a surprise cameo during Sabrina Carpenter's Coachella headline set on Friday night. It is a big moment for Sarandon, who has spoken publicly about feeling blacklisted in Hollywood over her pro-Palestine views.
articles.content:
Susan Sarandon walked out on the Coachella stage during Sabrina Carpenter's headline set on Friday and the crowd absolutely lost it. Sarandon is an Oscar-winning actress, best known for films like Thelma and Louise and Dead Man Walking, and she has been pretty open lately about feeling shut out of Hollywood work because of her public support for Palestine. Seeing her show up at one of the biggest stages in music right now felt like a statement.

Sabrina Carpenter is having a genuine cultural moment. After years of building a following, she broke through in a massive way and landing the Coachella headline slot in 2026 is proof of just how far she has come. Having Sarandon appear alongside her gave the set an extra layer of meaning that went beyond a typical celebrity cameo.

The pairing of a 79-year-old Oscar winner with a pop star in her mid-twenties is exactly the kind of unexpected crossover that Coachella lives for. Whether you care about the politics or just the spectacle, this was one of those moments that will be talked about long after the festival ends.
keyPoints: Susan Sarandon appeared as a surprise guest during Sabrina Carpenter's Coachella headline set on Friday
Sarandon has publicly said she feels blacklisted in Hollywood over her pro-Palestine views
Sabrina Carpenter is headlining Coachella 2026, a major milestone in her career
The cameo drew huge attention and is being widely shared online
It is one of the most talked about Coachella moments of the weekend so far

articles.title: Gucci Mane Fires Back at Pooh Shiesty on "Crash Dummy"
articles.summary: Gucci Mane has released a diss track called "Crash Dummy" aimed directly at Pooh Shiesty, who was arrested last week on robbery and kidnapping charges connected to an incident at a Dallas recording studio involving Gucci Mane. The track turns a shocking real-world event into one of rap's most talked-about beef moments of 2026.
articles.content:
Just over a week ago, news broke that Memphis rapper Pooh Shiesty and fellow rapper Big30 were arrested on robbery and kidnapping charges. The alleged incident happened at a recording studio in Dallas and directly involved Gucci Mane. That is a wild sentence to type, but here we are.

Gucci Mane, the Atlanta rap veteran who has been a dominant force in hip-hop since the mid-2000s, did not stay quiet. He responded with a diss track called "Crash Dummy," going straight at Pooh Shiesty and addressing the alleged robbery head on. Pooh Shiesty had been signed to Gucci Mane's 1017 Records label before their relationship soured, which gives this beef a particularly personal edge.

"Crash Dummy" is exactly the kind of rapid fire response that keeps rap beef feeling alive and unpredictable. Gucci Mane has been through legal battles and public conflicts before and always seems to come out swinging. Whether Pooh Shiesty responds from his current legal situation remains to be seen, but right now Gucci Mane has the mic.
keyPoints: Pooh Shiesty and Big30 were arrested last week on robbery and kidnapping charges involving Gucci Mane in Dallas
Gucci Mane responded with a diss track called "Crash Dummy" aimed at Pooh Shiesty
Pooh Shiesty was previously signed to Gucci Mane's 1017 Records label
The track addresses the alleged robbery directly making it both a musical and legal flashpoint
This is one of the biggest rap beef moments of 2026 so far

articles.title: Natasha Lyonne Says ICE Detained Her at Airport
articles.summary: Natasha Lyonne posted on X claiming she was detained by ICE after being removed from a flight following her appearance at the Euphoria Season 3 premiere on April 7. Homeland Security denied the claim, making this a very public and very confusing confrontation between a beloved celebrity and federal authorities.
articles.content:
Natasha Lyonne, the Emmy-nominated actress best known for Russian Doll and Orange Is the New Black, took to X on Friday to share something alarming. She said she was detained by ICE at an airport after being kicked off a flight. The incident reportedly happened after she attended the Euphoria Season 3 premiere in Hollywood on April 7.

Homeland Security pushed back fast and denied that any such detention took place. So right now there is a direct conflict between what Natasha Lyonne says happened and what the government says happened. That gap is exactly what has people talking. Natasha Lyonne is not someone who typically courts controversy, which makes this feel even more striking.

Euphoria Season 3 is already one of the most talked about TV events of 2026, so Natasha Lyonne appearing at its premiere was already a moment. Now this story has taken on a life of its own entirely separate from the show. Whatever the full truth turns out to be, this is a story worth watching closely.
keyPoints: Natasha Lyonne posted on X saying she was detained by ICE after being removed from a flight
The incident allegedly followed her April 7 appearance at the Euphoria Season 3 premiere
Homeland Security officially denied that any detention occurred
The conflict between Lyonne's account and the government's denial is the core of the story
Natasha Lyonne is an Emmy nominee known for Russian Doll and Orange Is the New Black

articles.title: Turnstile's Coachella Set Opens With Message From Brendan Yates' Father
articles.summary: Turnstile opened their Coachella set with a recorded message from William Yates, father of frontman Brendan Yates, just days after William Yates was attacked by former Turnstile guitarist Brady Ebert. The moment turned an already charged performance into something deeply personal and emotionally powerful.
articles.content:
Turnstile are a hardcore band from Baltimore who have quietly become one of the most exciting live acts around. Their Coachella 2026 set started in a way nobody expected. Instead of launching straight into music, the crowd heard a recorded message from William Yates, the father of Turnstile frontman Brendan Yates.

This matters because earlier in April, William Yates was physically attacked by Brady Ebert, who used to play guitar for Turnstile. Ebert is no longer in the band, and the details of the attack are still unfolding publicly. It is a genuinely shocking and upsetting situation, and the fact that Brendan Yates chose to open one of the biggest sets of his career with a message from his father says a lot about where his head is at right now.

Turnstile have built a fanbase that goes way beyond hardcore. Their album Glow On, released in 2021, brought them a whole new audience and their Coachella slot reflects that growth. This set was always going to be a big deal. The recording from William Yates made it unforgettable for a reason nobody could have anticipated.
keyPoints: Turnstile opened their Coachella 2026 set with a recorded message from Brendan Yates' father William Yates
William Yates was attacked earlier this month by former Turnstile guitarist Brady Ebert
The moment made an already anticipated set deeply personal
Turnstile are one of the biggest crossover acts from the hardcore scene right now
Brady Ebert is no longer a member of Turnstile

articles.title: A Waymo Went the Wrong Way Through a Whataburger Drive-Thru
articles.summary: A Waymo self-driving car went the wrong way through a Whataburger drive-thru, leaving a police officer so confused all he could do was wave his arms at it. The bizarre clip is the latest in a growing string of Waymo incidents that are starting to feel less funny and more concerning.
articles.content:
Picture this. A police officer is standing in a Whataburger parking lot. A Waymo driverless car rolls in, goes the wrong way through the drive-thru, and the officer's only option is to stand there waving his arms at a car that has no driver to respond to him. The footage is wild. Waymo is the self-driving car service owned by Alphabet, Google's parent company, and it operates robotaxi fleets in several US cities.

Waymo has had a run of public incidents lately, from cars blocking traffic to getting confused at intersections. Each clip goes viral and adds to a growing public debate about whether self-driving technology is actually ready for real streets with real people and real fast-food restaurants. A car that cannot navigate a Whataburger drive-thru without going the wrong way is a tough sell for the future of transport.

The image of a cop helplessly waving at a driverless car is genuinely funny until you think about what it represents. These vehicles are operating in public spaces right now. When something goes wrong, there is no driver to flag down, no one to hold accountable in the moment. That is the conversation this clip is quietly starting.
keyPoints: A Waymo self-driving car went the wrong way through a Whataburger drive-thru
A police officer on the scene could only wave his arms at the driverless car
Waymo is the self-driving robotaxi service run by Alphabet, Google's parent company
Waymo has had multiple public incidents recently that have gone viral
The clip raises real questions about whether self-driving cars are ready for everyday public spaces

articles.title: Anyma's Coachella Midnight Set Canceled by Strong Winds
articles.summary: Anyma, the Italian American DJ and one half of EDM duo Tale of Us, had his midnight Coachella main stage performance canceled on the first night of the festival due to strong wind conditions. It was one of the most anticipated closing sets of the weekend and its cancellation left thousands of fans disappointed.
articles.content:
Coachella's first night ended on a frustrating note for a lot of people. Anyma, who makes up one half of the acclaimed electronic music duo Tale of Us, was booked to close out the main stage at midnight. It was a massive slot. Anyma has built a reputation for deeply immersive, cinematic DJ sets that feel more like experiences than performances, and anticipation for this one was high.

Then the wind picked up. Festival organisers canceled the set due to strong wind conditions, citing safety concerns. These kinds of cancellations are always a gut punch for fans who have waited hours to get a good spot, and Anyma's set was genuinely one of the most talked-about bookings on the Coachella lineup going into the weekend.

Tale of Us, the duo Anyma and Camelphat collaborator Afterlife co-founded, is known for pushing electronic music into more artistic and emotional territory. Anyma's solo work has continued that tradition and his Coachella slot felt like a major mainstream breakthrough moment. Whether he gets a rescheduled set later in the weekend or has to wait until next year remains unclear, but this was a real loss for the festival's opening night.
keyPoints: Anyma's midnight Coachella main stage set on the first night was canceled due to strong wind conditions
Anyma is one half of the EDM duo Tale of Us alongside fellow artist Afterlife
The midnight slot was one of the most anticipated performances of Coachella weekend one
Safety concerns from the weather forced organisers to pull the plug on the performance
It is unclear whether Anyma will be given a rescheduled slot later in the festival

articles.title: FAA Wants Gamers to Solve Its Controller Crisis
articles.summary: The Federal Aviation Administration is looking at video game players as a potential talent pool to help fix a serious air traffic controller shortage across the United States. The US Government Accountability Office reported in January that controller numbers have been declining for years, making this an increasingly urgent problem.
articles.content:
The FAA has a big problem. There are not enough air traffic controllers in the US, and the numbers have been getting worse for years. The US Government Accountability Office flagged this in January, noting a steady decline in the workforce. Air traffic control is one of those jobs that requires intense focus, fast decision-making, and the ability to track multiple things at once under pressure.

Sound familiar? The FAA thinks gamers might actually have a head start on those skills. The idea is that people who play video games regularly, especially fast-paced games that demand spatial awareness and split-second reactions, could be naturally suited to learning air traffic control. It is not as far-fetched as it sounds. The military has used gaming-adjacent skills in recruitment for years.

This is a genuinely fascinating cultural moment. Gaming has spent decades being dismissed as a waste of time, and now a federal agency is saying those hours might qualify you for one of the most important jobs in national infrastructure. It is not a done deal, and there is a long training process involved no matter where recruits come from. But the fact that the FAA is even having this conversation says a lot about how seriously the shortage has become.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Federal Aviation Administration air traffic control
keyPoints: The FAA is exploring video gamers as potential recruits to address a growing air traffic controller shortage
The US Government Accountability Office reported in January that controller numbers have been declining for years
The FAA believes gaming skills like fast reactions and tracking multiple targets could translate well to the role
This is not the first time gaming skills have been linked to high-pressure professional roles
Recruits would still need to complete full official training regardless of their gaming background

articles.title: Coachella's Do LaB Stage Shut Down After Speaker Injures Attendee
articles.summary: The Do LaB stage at Coachella 2026 was reportedly closed after a speaker fell and injured a festivalgoer, with at least one attendee claiming a woman was badly hurt. There has been no official statement from Coachella or the Do LaB organizers yet.
articles.content:
The Do LaB is one of the most beloved spots at Coachella. It is a separate smaller stage run by the production company Do LaB, known for its late night electronic sets, wild light shows and a crowd that is fully committed to the party. It has been a Coachella fixture for years and attracts a devoted following every weekend.

On Friday night, reports started circulating that the Do LaB stage had been shut down after a speaker fell and hit a festivalgoer. At least one person at the scene claimed a woman was badly injured. That is a serious situation and understandably it has people concerned. As of Saturday morning there has been no official word from Coachella or Do LaB about what exactly happened or the condition of anyone involved.

Festival safety is always a big conversation, and incidents like this tend to prompt wider questions about how live events manage their setups under pressure. Coachella draws hundreds of thousands of people across two weekends and any injury on site gets a lot of attention fast. If and when an official update comes through, it will matter a lot for how the rest of Weekend 1 and all of Weekend 2 plays out.
articles.wikiSearchQuery: Coachella Valley Music and Arts Festival
keyPoints: The Do LaB stage at Coachella 2026 was reportedly closed after a speaker fell onto a festivalgoer
At least one eyewitness claimed a woman was badly injured
No official statement has been released by Coachella or Do LaB as of Saturday morning
The Do LaB is a fan favourite smaller stage known for electronic music and late night sets
The incident is raising fresh questions about festival safety

articles.title: MUBI Lost 200,000 Subscribers After 2025 PR Nightmare
articles.summary: MUBI CEO Efe Cakarel has revealed that the arthouse streaming platform lost 200,000 subscribers following a damaging 2025 PR crisis, with the commercial failure of the film Die My Love contributing to layoffs and negative cash flow. This is a rare moment of radical honesty from a streaming CEO and it paints a tough picture for one of cinema's most beloved indie platforms.
articles.content:
MUBI is the streaming platform for people who love serious, artsy cinema. It built a loyal following by championing films that big platforms ignore. But MUBI CEO Efe Cakarel has now confirmed publicly that the platform had a rough 2025. He disclosed that MUBI lost 200,000 subscribers in the wake of what he called a PR nightmare that hit the company last year.

Part of the fallout came from the poor performance of Die My Love, a film MUBI backed that failed to connect commercially. The losses were serious enough that MUBI had to conduct layoffs and the company entered negative cash flow. For a platform that has always positioned itself as the thoughtful alternative to Netflix, this is a significant moment of reckoning.

Efe Cakarel choosing to be this open about the numbers is unusual for any streaming executive. Most companies bury this kind of data. The fact that MUBI is being transparent suggests the platform is trying to reset and rebuild trust with its audience. If you care about independent cinema having a home online, MUBI's recovery matters.
keyPoints: MUBI lost 200,000 subscribers following a 2025 PR crisis
CEO Efe Cakarel disclosed the losses publicly, which is rare for a streaming executive
The film Die My Love flopped commercially and contributed to the financial damage
MUBI conducted layoffs and experienced negative cash flow
MUBI is a streaming platform focused on arthouse and independent cinema
